description: Correcting a balance by writing a new value in place, destroying the history needed to audit or reverse the change.
body: Failure mode: a balance is fixed by UPDATE-ing the balance column to a new number rather than posting a compensating entry, so there is no record of what changed, why, or by whom, and the change cannot be audited or cleanly reversed. Detection signals: balance columns mutated directly; no ledger entry explaining a balance change; reconciliation cannot trace a delta. Do not edit balances in place. Safer fallback: post an immutable adjusting/reversal entry with actor and reason and derive the balance from entries. Risk boundary: advisory only; any direct balance mutation must be denied and escalated to a human with an audit trail, never performed silently.
